data.title: U.S. 30-Year Treasury Auction Came in StrongMarket Talk
data.description: The U.S. Treasury's auction of 30-year bonds on Thursday was strong, coming in rich versus the secondary market, ING rates strategists say in a note. "The indirect bid was strong (mostly foreign). The direct bid (more domestic) too," they say. Auction metrics were good, and "this is a vote of positivity on Treasuries," the strategists say. A perception of value likely helped as the 30-year yield rose in the hours into the auction, they say. "Still, a strong 30-year auction following a strong 10-year auction is quite a good look, especially in the wake of a really poor 3-year auction where central banks were expected to show up and lap up paper." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: SNB spokesperson declines to comment on Swiss franc strength
data.description:
At this stage, they're put in a tough spot. With markets facing extreme turbulence and dislocations, the franc has been one of the key beneficiaries - and still is. It will be a waste to burn their reserves in fighting back until the fundamental landscape changes.

But the longer this persists and the more the franc strengthens, the likelihood of a return to negative interest rate policy is ever increasing for Switzerland.

data.title: Democrats slam DOJ’s ‘grave mistake’ in disbanding crypto crime unit
data.description:
Crypto-critical US Senator Elizabeth Warren has led six Senate Democrats in urging the Department of Justice to reverse its decision to terminate its crypto investigations and prosecutions division.

In an April 10 letter to Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, the Senators said the decision to disband the department’s National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team was a “grave mistake” that would support “sanctions evasion, drug trafficking, scams, and child sexual exploitation.”

Senators Richard Durbin, Mazie Hirono, Sheldon Whitehouse, Christopher Coons and Richard Blumenthal signed the letter in addition to Warren.

On April 7, Blanche shuttered the DOJ’s crypto enforcement team, saying in a memo that “The Department of Justice is not a digital assets regulator.”

The senators claim that the decision gave a “free pass to cryptocurrency money launderers” and claimed that crypto mixing services — used to obfuscate blockchain transactions — are “go-to tools for cybercriminals.” 

“It makes no sense for DOJ to announce a hands-off approach to tools that are being used to support such terrible crimes,” the letter said.

The senators also questioned why the Justice Department had decided not to prosecute a “host of crimes involving digital assets, including violations of the Bank Secrecy Act.”

They claimed that this creates a “systemic vulnerability in the digital assets sector,” which “drug traffickers, terrorists, fraudsters, and adversaries” will exploit on a large scale. 

The lawmakers requested a staff-level briefing no later than May 1, providing “detailed information on the rationale behind these decisions.” 

Targeting Trump family crypto endeavors 

The letter also took a swipe at the Trump family’s crypto projects, suggesting potential conflicts of interest.

A press release accompanying the letter stated that the senators are raising concerns about the “potential connections” between the DOJ’s actions and the crypto ventures of President Donald Trump and his family.

The Trumps have an interest in and have backed the crypto platform World Liberty Financial along with its token. The platform is also planning to launch a stablecoin while President Trump’s sons, Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr., are working to launch a crypto-mining company called American Bitcoin.

“Your decisions give rise to concerns that President Trump’s interest in selling his cryptocurrency may be the reason for easing law enforcement scrutiny,” the Democrats stated.

In a memo announcing the crypto enforcement team’s disbandment, Blanche accused the Biden administration of using the Justice Department to “pursue a reckless strategy of regulation by prosecution.”

data.title: Cambi ASA: Key information relating to a proposed capital repayment
data.description:
Asker, Norway, 11 April 2025

The Board of Directors of Cambi ASA proposes the following capital repaymentforthe financial year 2024:

o Dividend amount: 0.30o Declared currency: NOKo Last day including right: 9 May 2025o Ex-date: 12 May 2025o Record date: 13 May 2025o Payment date: on or about 20 May 2025o Date of approval: 8 May 2025 at the annual general meeting

This information is published in accordance with Cambi ASA's continuingobligations as a listed company set out in the Norwegian Securities TradingActsection 5-12.

For more information, please contact: Mats Tristan Tjemsland, CFO, mats.tjemsland@cambi.com, +47 920 67 094

About Cambi

Cambi is a global supplier of biogas technology and solutions for wastewatertreatment plants and anaerobic digestion facilities. Since 1992, Cambi hasretained market leadership through continuous innovation and a robustportfolioof proven and patented technologies. With strong project delivery and customersupport capabilities, the company has delivered many well-performinginstallations in 28 countries on six continents.

Cambi's thermal hydrolysis process increases renewable energy output, reducesoperational costs, protects public health, and minimises greenhouse gasemissions. It is a future-proof solution suitable for all biosolids outlets,including land application and thermal processes such as drying, pyrolysis,andincineration. Thermal hydrolysis is compatible with all sludge and wastetreatment regulations and can be integrated smoothly at new and existinganaerobic digestion sites.

Cambi is listed on Euronext Growth Oslo, a multilateral trading facility inEuronext, Europe's largest stock exchange platform. Find out more atcambi.com.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/643574

data.title: RAK Insurance to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Ras Al Khaimah National Insurance (ADX:RAKNIC), d/b/a RAK Insurance, will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 12:30 pm local time on April 17 due to its board meeting.

The United Arab Emirates-based insurer will resume trading after its meeting results are disclosed, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch April 11
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Friday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 2 hour):

ECOFIN MEETING

An informal meeting of EU ministers for economic and financial affairs and central bank governors will be held on Friday.

The informal ECOFIN Council will focus on defense financing, summarizing the discussions on this topic held since the start of the Polish presidency. Other key topics will include financial markets (with Central Bank Governors) and the current geopolitical situation, the Polish presidency informed.

EMBARGO ON POLISH POULTRY

Due to the bird flu outbreak, the European Commission wants to introduce a ban on importing poultry from Poland, Gazeta Wyborcza reported, citing unofficial information.

CONSTRUCTION CONTRACTS

Organizations representing Polish companies in the construction industry are demanding that the Minister of Infrastructure increase the indexation limit from the current 15% to 21% of road construction contracts concluded before the outbreak of the war in Ukraine, Rzeczpospolita daily reported.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

For other related news, double click on:

Polish equities E.Europe equities

Polish money Polish debt

Eastern Europe All emerging markets

Hot stocks Stock markets

Market debt news Forex news

For real-time index quotes, double click on:

Warsaw WIG20 Budapest BUX Prague PX

data.title: Trump's Tariff Pause Is Fragile, France's Macron Says
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

A pause on trade tariffs between Europe and the U.S. is welcome but fragile, French President Emmanuel Macron said.

The EU suspended its own package of retaliatory tariffs after President Trump on Wednesday set out a 90-day pause to the bulk of his planned import duties, which would have meant a broad 20% tariff on all goods brought in from the EU.

That suspension is "a signal and a gateway to negotiations," Macron said in a post on X published on Friday.

"But this pause is fragile," he said.

European firms remain mired in uncertainty, and U.S. tariffs remain on autos and steel produced in the bloc, Macron noted.

"With the [European] Commission, and in a spirit of European unity, our goal is simple: to negotiate the withdrawal of these unfair tariffs and ensure a balanced deal, without asymmetry on either side," Macron said.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise, Could Gain FurtherMarket Talk
data.description:
0547 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields are slightly higher in early European hours after sharp gyrations during the week on headlines around U.S. tariffs. "Our impression from here is the 10-year Treasury yield will have a tendency to head for the 4.5% area, potentially with a view to thinking about 4.75%," ING rates strategists say in a note. Directionally, there seems to be no mood to materially test lower in yield right now, despite the open door being presented by the risk-off backdrop, they say. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 5.5 basis points, last trading at 4.446%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0536 GMT - The Bank of Japan's latest consumer survey shows households continue to feel the pinch from rising prices, signaling that it's difficult for a virtuous cycle of income, spending and inflation to work smoothly in Japan. The quarterly survey shows nearly 56% say their living conditions are getting tougher, with more than 90% of them saying that's due to higher prices. Only 3.9% say their living conditions were easy. Policymakers hope wage growth will encourage more household spending, but the results suggest that consumer sentiment may not turn positive so quickly. Some 86.7% of the respondents say prices would increase further, either slightly or significantly, a year from now, according to the survey. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0535 GMT - Singapore's advance data is likely to show the economy grew 4.6% on year in 1Q, according to the median estimate of five economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal. That compares with a 5.0% growth in 4Q of 2024. 1Q GDP growth is likely to be robust, says SMBC's Jeff Ng says in an email. Policymakers have ruled out retaliation against the U.S.' 10% reciprocal tariff on imports from Singapore, but are reassessing downside risks to the economy, says the head of Asia Macro Strategy. The advance data is also tipped to show that GDP was flat on quarter on a seasonally adjusted basis, according to the poll. That would be down from 0.5% expansion in 4Q. The data are due Monday. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0524 GMT - The European Central Bank could cut interest rates more aggressively than currently priced in by money markets, although this might require a ​significant worsening in economic data, according to SEB Research. "We continue to predict that the ECB cuts the deposit facility rate below neutral, to 1.50% in September," it says. SEB Research's ECB view has received support from recent events around tariffs but continued back-to-back rate cuts need support from data in the coming months. Money markets currently price the terminal deposit rate, where the ECB stops rate cuts, at 1.75%, to be reached by October, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0512 GMT - Chinese leader Xi Jinping's planned visit to Malaysia could be aimed at garnering more support from Asean countries amid rising U.S.-China tensions, says Hong Leong IB analyst Ng Jun Sheng, noting that Malaysia holds the chairmanship of the bloc this year. The visit offers Malaysia a strategic chance to balance ties with both China and the U.S. but will also tests its diplomatic acumen, he says. Stronger China ties could boost Malaysia's regional role and attract investment, he says. However, trade complexities, especially around potential new U.S. tariffs, must be navigated carefully, he warns. Xi's visit may also serve as a soft power push, with China offering cooperation in digital tech, infrastructure, and renewables, he says. Recent MOUs are seen as goodwill to foster closer ties, he adds. Xi will make a state visit to Malaysia on April 15-17. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0453 GMT - Chinese President Xi's upcoming visit to Malaysia comes as the Southeast Asian country stands in the crossfire of worsening tensions between Beijing and Washington. Lee Heng Guie, executive director at the Socio-Economic Research Center--a Malaysian think tank--thinks Kuala Lumpur is likely to maintain a neutral stance, looking to remain friendly with both the U.S. and China, while hoping Beijing will help narrow the trade gap with Malaysia by importing more palm oil and fruit. Trump's "unreasonable" tariffs have hit Southeast Asia particularly hard, fueling perceptions that the region is more aligned with China, its largest trading partner, he adds. Lee sees potential for China to boost investment in AI, digitalization, and renewable energy. Xi will make a state visit to Malaysia from April 15-17. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0356 GMT - Fresh headwinds prompt Barclays economists to further cut growth forecasts for Thailand. The bank now lowers its 2025 GDP growth view for Thailand to 2.0% after tariff expectations had spurred it to cut growth forecast to 2.5% in March. Despite some relief from the 90-day delay in reciprocal U.S. tariffs, Thailand still faces multiple threats, says Shreya Sodhani. The trade turmoil could spur more cheap Chinese products to flood Thai markets and hit Thai exports directly. The recent earthquake has also hit confidence among tourists, especially from China, she says. Barclays expects fewer arrivals this year, leading to a 10bp drop in its overall growth forecast. Growth concerns will likely force the central bank to deliver three more cuts this year, it says. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0355 GMT - The Bank of Korea is expected to stand pat on its monetary policy next week. Twenty out of 25 economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal expect the BOK to hold its base rate steady at 2.75% at its policy meeting on Thursday, with the remaining five penciling in another quarter-percentage-point cut following its easing move in February. Despite a rising need to loosen monetary policy settings further to support growth, uncertainty over U.S. tariffs amid forex market volatility will likely keep the BOK in a wait-and-see mode, some economists say. Most economists expect the BOK to lower growth forecasts and cut its policy rate in May. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0350 GMT - The Chinese economy likely had a solid start to the year, signaling that Beijing's annual target of around-5% growth is on track, according to a Wall Street Journal poll of economists. China's 1Q GDP is tipped to have expanded 5.2% on the year despite the tariff turmoil. Economists expect a boost from Beijing's proactive fiscal policy approach, with the issuance of treasury bonds reaching a record high during January-March. Other data due next week could be mixed. Industrial output growth for March is seen at 5.7% compared with January-February's 5.9%, with growth in retail sales and fixed-asset investments expected at 4.4% and 4.1% respectively compared to 4.0% and 4.1%. Economists widely expect Beijing to accelerate stimulus in the coming months as U.S. tariffs bite. The data are due Wednesday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

0328 GMT - China's outbound shipments were likely resilient in March despite U.S. tariffs, thanks partly to favorable base effects, according to a WSJ poll of economists. Chinese exports likely rose 4.4% on year, compared with the 2.3% increase in January-February, the poll shows. Beijing combines economic data for the first two months of the year to iron out distortions caused by shifts in the timing of the Lunar New Year holiday. Economists say the growth was aided by exporters' front-loading of shipments to get ahead of potentially higher tariffs. China's imports likely rose 1.8% last month, compared with the 8.4% drop in January-February. The country's March trade surplus is estimated at $77 billion versus $170.5 billion in January-February. The trade data are due Monday. (singaporeeditors@wsj.com)

0327 GMT - U.S. tariff hikes are set to disrupt corporate supply chains and earnings in Asia-Pacific despite the 90-day pause on some of the larger increases, says Fitch Ratings. Effective tariff rates on China have been raised further and aren't subject to the 90-day pause. This, coupled with the minimum 10% tariff on all APAC exports to the U.S., will hurt companies that sell to the U.S. and Asia's export-oriented economies, Fitch says in a note. The ratings firm sees the auto, tech hardware, chemicals and metals and mining sectors as most exposed to tariff risks. Once the April 2 tariff hikes are implemented in full, exports from Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, Taiwan, India, South Korea, Malaysia and Japan could face much higher tariffs, hurting growth and dimming corporate earnings prospects, Fitch adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0311 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session on falling short-dated Treasury yields, which typically diminish the appeal of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. There's been a weakening in U.S. assets and better expectations elsewhere such as the possibility of more EU fiscal support, Maybank analysts say in a forex research & strategy report. In the near term, U.S. dollar weakness could persist as these factors may hold up, the analysts say. USD/SGD falls 0.3% to 1.3280. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Aurionpro Solutions to Acquire Fintra Software
data.description:
Technology services provider Aurionpro Solutions has agreed to acquire a 100% stake in Fintra Software for up to 230 million Indian rupees, according to a Friday filing to the Indian bourses.

The proposed transaction includes the acquisition of all the intellectual property rights and resources of Fintra.

Fintra is an information technology company that provides software products and services for the banking

and financial services industry, with a focus on wholesale banking solutions.

The acquisition is expected to be completed within 30 days.

Shares of the company were up nearly 8% in recent trade.

data.title: IMF, Sri Lanka in Ongoing Talks Over $2.9 Billion Deal
data.description:
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) said in a statement on Friday that it is working with Sri Lanka to complete the fourth review of its $2.9 billion loan program, following a team's recent visit to evaluate progress.

The global lender commended Sri Lanka's reform agenda for achieving "commendable outcomes," citing 5% economic growth in 2024, a 2.6% decline in consumer prices during March 2025, and an increase in official reserves to $6.5 billion.

However, the IMF warned that a recent external shock and elevated global uncertainty could weigh on the recovery.

It emphasized the importance of sustaining reform momentum, improving tax compliance, avoiding new tax exemptions, and restoring cost-recovery electricity pricing.

The IMF also urged better-targeted social spending to protect the vulnerable and called for prudent budget execution to preserve limited fiscal space.

data.title: India's Olectra Greentech rises on 4 bln-rupee order win
data.description:
** Olectra Greentech rises 4.55% to 1,139.6 rupees

** Electric bus manufacturer wins 4.2 bln-rupee ($48.8 mln) order from Himachal Pradesh state road transport corporation to supply and maintain 297 electric buses

** Buses will be delivered over 11 months

** OLEC narrows 12-month decline to 38.4%

data.title: Nomura sees pause in US reciprocal tariffs to benefit Indian auto ancillary firms
data.description:
** Analysts at Nomura believe the pause in U.S. reciprocal tariffs and escalating trade war between the U.S. and China can benefit Indian auto ancillary companies

** This supply chain restructuring can lead to strong gains for players such as Sona BLW , Sansera Engineering , Bharat Forge and Samvardhana Motherson - Nomura

** The shares climb 4%, 4.8%, 1.4% and 3%, respectively

** U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday temporarily paused reciprocal tariffs beyond 10% on most countries except China

** However, fully built imported autos will continue to have 25% duty while auto parts will be subject to 25% duty from May 3

** Brokerage sees lower interest rates, lower income taxes, potential cuts in oil prices to limit the downside risks in the auto ancillary space

** Since Trump's announcement of tariffs on April 2, SONB has fallen 12.5%, SASE has lost 14.5%, BFRG down 15.9% and SAMD has declined 14.5%, as of Wednesday's close

data.title: United Fidelity Insurance to Halt Trading Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
United Fidelity Insurance (ADX:FIDELITYUNITED) shares will pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 1 pm local time on April 30 due to its annual general meeting.

The United Arab Emirates-based insurer will resume trading after its meeting results are published, according to a Friday release.

data.title: REG-Trifork secures landmark project to transform Oman’s healthcare system
data.description:
Press release

Trifork secures landmark project to transform Oman’s healthcare system

Muscat, 11 April 2025 – The Ministry of Health in Oman has selected Trifork to develop a state-of-the-art Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) system while integrating with the National Health Information Exchange (NHER), which in parallel will be upgraded by Trifork during the project. This project represents a significant milestone in modernizing Oman’s healthcare system in alignment with Oman Vision 2040.

After a competitive bidding process involving six contenders, Trifork was selected for its more than 20 years of expertise in Digital Health, which has been demonstrated through successful projects in Switzerland and Denmark and its strong international profile.

Strengthening Oman’s healthcare system

The project aims to upgrade Oman’s healthcare systems. The benefits of the new system include improved cost recovery, allowing government providers to reclaim insurance companies' expenses more efficiently, faster claims processing, and reduced waiting times for patients at Ministry of Health facilities, which are key steps toward a more patient-focused healthcare experience.

Key phases and deliverables

The project is structured into phases, with gradual implementation over two years. The initial proof of concept will be completed in six months, followed by a gradual implementation of core functionalities, ensuring that the benefits of the solutions are implemented as soon as possible.

These milestones align with the Ministry of Health’s digitalization strategy, which focuses on enhancing healthcare efficiency, data-driven decision-making, and seamless patient care through advanced technology. They also support Oman Vision 2040’s broader goals of leveraging digital transformation to improve public services, strengthen healthcare infrastructure, and drive sustainable national development.

Strategic partnership

Trifork Oman brings invaluable expertise from similar engagements across Europe to the project. By integrating advanced solutions and leveraging global best practices, the company will deliver a tailored system that meets the unique needs of Oman’s healthcare ecosystem.

The Ministry of Health in Oman oversees 263 health institutions, including 50 hospitals (4,954 beds), 21 health complexes, and 192 health centers. In 2022, they recorded 14.9 million outpatient visits – about 41,000 daily. Serving over 5 million people, the ministry prioritizes accessible, high-quality care and advances digital transformation under Oman’s Vision 2040.

Commitment to innovation in Oman

"This contract represents a major milestone for Trifork Oman in our ambition to contribute to the Sultanate’s goals for digital transformation and innovation. We are honored to use our strong expertise in digital health to contribute to the ongoing innovation in Oman’s healthcare sector and see this as the start of a long partnership," says Christian Hemmingsen, CEO of Trifork Oman.

Investor and media contact

Frederik Svanholm

Group Investment Director, Head of IR & PR

frsv@trifork.com, +41 79 357 7317

About Trifork

Trifork is a pioneering global technology partner, empowering enterprise and public sector customers with innovative solutions. With 1,229 professionals across 73 business units in 16 countries, Trifork delivers expertise in inspiring, building, and running advanced software solutions across diverse sectors, including public administration, healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, energy, financial services, retail, and real estate. Trifork Labs, the Group’s R&D hub, drives innovation by investing in and developing synergistic and high-potential technology companies. Trifork Group AG is a publicly listed company on Nasdaq Copenhagen. Learn more at trifork.com.

data.title: Notice of annual general meeting 2025 in Cambi ASA
data.description:
Asker, Norway, 11 April 2025

Cambi ASA's annual general meeting will be held on Thursday, 8 May 2025 at10:00am CEST. The meeting will be conducted digitally via Orgbrain.

The attached notice includes information on the agenda, voting procedures,registration of attendance, proxy appointments and advance voting.Shareholdersare encouraged to use Orgbrain for registering their attendance and submittingvotes or proxies. Shareholders who do not have an email address registered inthe Norwegian Central Securities Depository (VPS) as of the record date maycontact Cambi at investors@cambi.com to receive access.

The record date is set to 30 April 2025, and only shareholders registered intheVPS at that time are entitled to attend and vote at the general meeting. Forease of administration, Cambi kindly asks that registrations, advance votes,orproxies be submitted no later than Wednesday, 7 May 2025 at 16:00 CEST.

The annual report for the financial year 2024 is available on Cambi's investorpages: investors.cambi.com/results-and-reports.

This information is published in accordance with Cambi ASA's continuingobligations as a listed company set out in the Norwegian Securities TradingActsection 5-12.

For more information, please contact: o Mats Tristan Tjemsland, CFO, mats.tjemsland@cambi.com, +47 920 67 094o Dragos Talvescu, Senior Corporate Relations Manager,dragos.talvescu@cambi.com, +47 907 99 522

About Cambi

Cambi is a global supplier of biogas technology and solutions for wastewatertreatment plants and anaerobic digestion facilities. Since 1992, Cambi hasretained market leadership through continuous innovation and a robustportfolioof proven and patented technologies. With strong project delivery and customersupport capabilities, the company has delivered many well-performinginstallations in 28 countries on six continents.

Cambi's thermal hydrolysis process increases renewable energy output, reducesoperational costs, protects public health, and minimises greenhouse gasemissions. It is a future-proof solution suitable for all biosolids outlets,including land application and thermal processes such as drying, pyrolysis,andincineration. Thermal hydrolysis is compatible with all sludge and wastetreatment regulations and can be integrated smoothly at new and existinganaerobic digestion sites.

Cambi is listed on Euronext Growth Oslo, a multilateral trading facility inEuronext, Europe's largest stock exchange platform. Find out more atcambi.com.

data.title: Back to white-knuckle ride in the markets
data.description:
BACK TO WHITE-KNUCKLE RIDE IN THE MARKETS

Fears of a sharp downturn in the global economy have sent markets convulsing once more, with action focused on currencies and bonds as the escalating U.S.-China trade war has investors throwing out the usual playbook and fleeing dollar-based assets.

Stock futures in Europe were pointing to a subdued open but the Swiss franc reached a 10-year high and the yen was its strongest in six months. Gold prices resumed their march to successive record highs and the euro rose to levels not seen since February 2022.

King dollar no more, perhaps.

After an all-too-brief relief rally that followed U.S. President Donald Trump's temporary retreat from some of his tariff threats, Asian stock markets from Japan to Australia were showing a sea of red. Stock markets in China, the remaining target of Trump's wrath, were nevertheless relatively steady.

Relentless selling resumed in the dollar and bonds, with the 10-year note yield rising to 4.444% and on course for its biggest weekly increase since 2001, LSEG data showed. Thirty-year bond yields (US30YT=RR) are set for their biggest weekly jump since at least 1982.

This week's volatile reaction in the bond market has called into question Treasury bonds' status as the world's safest asset. So, what are the safe assets now?

Apart from the surge in currencies such as the yen and Swiss franc, gold prices have lurched to another record above $3,200 per ounce. The yellow metal's rise shows no signs of stopping as safe haven flows keep on coming.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

Economic events: March inflation data for Germany, Feb GDP estimate for UK, tariff updates

Trying to keep up with the latest tariff news?

Our new daily news digest offers a rundown of the top market-moving headlines impacting global trade. Sign up for Tariff Watch here.

(Ankur Banerjee)

*****

data.description:

Computer parts maker Logitech International on Thursday withdrew its 2026 forecast, citing continued uncertainty stemming from U.S. President Donald Trump's trade policies.

data.title: European Markets Set for Cautiously Higher Open
data.description:
European equity markets were poised to open slightly higher on Friday, as investors looked to wrap up a volatile week dominated by tariff-related turmoil.

The European Union temporarily paused its planned countermeasures against US tariffs for 90 days, aligning with Washington’s own reprieve in an effort to give negotiations a chance.

Still, caution prevailed as the US raised tariffs on Chinese goods to a cumulative 145%, stoking fears of a prolonged trade war and weighing on market sentiment.

Uncertainty over the outcome of global trade talks also continued to cloud the outlook.

In premarket trading, Euro Stoxx 50 futures rose 0.6%, while Stoxx 600 futures gained 0.3%.

data.title: Fujian Funeng Forecasts Up to 47% Rise in Q1 Profit
data.description:
Fujian Funeng expected to record first-quarter annual profit growth of between 39% and 47% amid higher wind power generation.

Q1 net profit attributable to shareholders is expected to range between 733.5 million yuan and 771.1 million yuan, compared with 526.4 million yuan the previous year, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the power company were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Envision Greenwise Buys Stake in Singaporean Lithium Battery Waste Management Firm; Shares Rise 5%
data.description:
Envision Greenwise purchased the entire stake in Green Jade Reverse Logistics from Jade Destiny for HK$35.0 million, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The consideration will be paid through the issuance of up to 4,545,455 new shares at HK$7.70 apiece once the transaction is completed, the filing said.

The shares comprise 0.33% of the entire issued share capital of Envision Greenwise as enlarged by the issuance.

Green Jade Reverse Logistics is a Singapore-based lithium battery waste handling business.

Shares in Envision Greenwise jumped 5% during Friday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Manazel to Pause Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Manazel (ADX:MANAZEL) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 9 am local time on April 30 due to its annual general meeting.

The Abu Dhabi-listed real estate company will resume trading after it publishes its meeting results, according to a Thursday release published Friday.

data.title: FNC INTERVIEW WITH SEN. ERIC SCHMITT (R-MO)
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 11, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

SEN. ERIC SCHMITT (R-MO)

FNC INTERVIEW WITH SEN. ERIC SCHMITT (R-MO)

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

FNC INTERVIEW WITH SEN. ERIC SCHMITT (R-MO)

APRIL 11, 2025

SPEAKERS:

SEN. ERIC SCHMITT (R-MO), LAURA INGRAHAM, FNC HOST

LAURA INGRAHAM, FOX NEWS HOST: Joining me now, Missouri Senator Eric Schmitt. Senator, well, here we go. We got a massive problem with illegal immigration in the United States. The voters made it clear they wanted Donald Trump to do something about it. Donald Trump tries to take measures, sometimes, I guess extraordinary measures to keep us safe. And yet the courts say no, we're going to step in as the commander-in-chief and essentially do your job.

SEN. ERIC SCHMITT (R-MO): Well, I think it's important to give context here, Laura, as this is breaking, I just had a chance to review the order before coming on your show, and it's very limited. This guy is deportable. This guy is a gang member. He is an MS-13 member, and they can deport him. They just couldn't deport him to El Salvador evidently.

So at the end of the day, this guy is going to be gone, and he should be gone, and there are hundreds of thousands more like him. This mass deportation effort, the American people, as you said, voted for this. We need more of it. And Judge Boasberg was slapped down earlier this week when he tried to intervene in the core Article 2 powers of the President of the United States. So as all these cases make their way through, the President is well within his authority to do it. In this specific example that just happened here, he's going to get deported, and he should it just will be somewhere else, other than El Salvador.

INGRAHAM: So he'll be brought back to the United States, kept in immigration custody, presumably, and then deported to his home country. You think that's how it's going to work?

SCHMITT: So actually, he's from El Salvador. He had a credible claim under the Biden administration for asylum under evidently a threat from another gang, but he is a hardened criminal, so they can deport him because he's deportable. I mean, it's how ridiculous all this is. He's deportable. They will deport him somewhere else that they just can't actually deport him to El Salvador. So either way, the Trump administration is going to continue this effort. And like I said, there are over a million people here with criminal backgrounds that — on the terrorist watchlist. The Biden administration led in, whether through a goofy asylum process where they gave them a court date 10 years later or through this parole process.

Either way, they were just trying to allow as many people to come here illegally, 15 to 20 million. So this is going to be methodical. The good news is, border crossings are down 95 percent, but there's a lot more work to do on these actual deportations.

INGRAHAM: Well, and also broke right before the show, and DOGE was tweeting this out as well that apparently there are 6300 individuals who were paroled into the United States. That's their only legal status. Is that Biden's people thought, oh, let's bring in these people. It turns out these individuals were of the 6300 I'm talking about, not the overall graphic, but these individuals were actually on a terrorist, an FBI terrorist watchlist, so 6300 should not have been brought in here, but there was no vetting that. So they were on this FBI terror tracking list. So again, this is what we're learning now about what Biden did to America.

SCHMITT: Well, it's worse than that, because these are just the people we know about who were paroled. There's literally tens of thousands of others that came in here illegally on the gotaways. But in this example, it just goes to show the abuse of the Biden administration. The parole process is supposed to be individualized, right? An individual case seeking to come here, somebody wanting to come here, maybe here temporarily, and then they go home eventually, right?

Biden used this to parole people from entire countries and kept extending that and extending that, violating the law. So this is, again, all this kind of two-step that they use to flood this country through mass migration, through the NGOs who are being funded by USAID. This whole thing is a disaster, but president Trump is bringing order out of the chaos here and the deportation — the deportations are going to continue.

But this is just another example, Laura. These people came here legally. They were given parole. They should have been individualized. They weren't individualized, and now they're receiving government benefits. They need to be cut off and sent back.

INGRAHAM: Yeah. Well, again, the Customs and Border Protection, they're getting to the bottom of these hundreds of thousands of people who were — who were flown into the United States.

SCHMITT: Yeah.

INGRAHAM: Okay. They weren't here, they were flown into the United States, which a lot of Americans don't understand. But again, just so far, they found 6,300 of them on this FBI terrorist screening list.

SCHMITT: So far.

INGRAHAM: I mean, this can't even — we're beyond the Supreme Court, guys. We're beyond the Supreme Court. We're on — I think Americans have to understand just how endangered our country is, not just by the economic waste, fraud, and abuse, but by potential terrorists who are just living amongst us. Senator, will the U.S. Senate conduct hearings into this particular issue?

SCHMITT: Absolutely, we should. And we got to do everything we can to provide the oversight to make sure they're continuing to find these people. But, Laura, this is the open borders crowd. These are the same people that the globalist that that suppress free speech. They don't believe in those things. They don't believe in borders. They think they're arbitrary lines on maps, and they use our taxpayer dollars to fly them in here legally, and we need to get them out of here.

INGRAHAM: All right, senator. Thank you very much.

END

data.title: Michiel Havermans, SVP Cloetta International, to leave Cloetta
data.description:
Cloetta AB

Stockholm, Sweden - Cloetta, Northern Europe's leading confectionery company, announces that Michiel Havermans, Senior Vice President Cloetta International, and member of the Group Management Team, has decided to leave Cloetta at the beginning of June 2025 after seven years at the company.

“I would like to thank Michiel for his contribution to the development of Cloetta during the past years. He has during our joint time in the Group Management Team been a passionate team player and has together with his team developed our international and UK business. I would sincerely like to thank Michiel for his dedication to Cloetta over the past seven years and wish him all the best in his new challenges outside our company.”, says Katarina Tell, President and CEO.

“I have enjoyed my time at Cloetta and I’m proud of the work done and results achieved in International Markets and the UK during my time at the company.” says Michiel Havermans, outgoing SVP Cloetta International.

Cloetta will provide an update on the long-term solution for Michiel’s responsibilities in due time, while he continues in his role for two more months.

Investor and media contact

Laura Lindholm, Director, Communications & Investor Relations

Cambi ASA has today published its annual report and sustainability report forthe financial year 2024. Both documents are attached and available on thecompany's website: investors.cambi.com/results-and-reports.The annual report presents financial results, business developments and riskmanagement, while the sustainability report describes Cambi's work on ESGtopics, including environmental impact, workplace safety and human rights duediligence, as well as corporate governance and accountability.

About Cambi

Cambi is a global supplier of biogas technology and solutions for wastewatertreatment plants and anaerobic digestion facilities. Since 1992, Cambi hasretained market leadership through continuous innovation and a robustportfolioof proven and patented technologies. With strong project delivery and customersupport capabilities, the company has delivered many well-performinginstallations in 28 countries on six continents.

Cambi's thermal hydrolysis process increases renewable energy output, reducesoperational costs, protects public health, and minimises greenhouse gasemissions. It is a future-proof solution suitable for all biosolids outlets,including land application and thermal processes such as drying, pyrolysis,andincineration. Thermal hydrolysis is compatible with all sludge and wastetreatment regulations and can be integrated smoothly at new and existinganaerobic digestion sites.

Cambi is listed on Euronext Growth Oslo, a multilateral trading facility inEuronext, Europe's largest stock exchange platform. Find out more atcambi.com.
